text.title: RACI Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Worked RACI examples for a feature development team and a solopreneur setup, plus common antipatterns and agent-specific pitfalls.

example.code: Task | PM | Product | Engineering | Design | QA --------------------|----|---------|--------------|---------|----| Define requirements | C | A | C | C | I Create mockups | I | C | I | R | I Approve design | I | A | C | R | I Write code | I | C | R | I | I Code review | I | I | A | I | I Write tests | I | I | C | I | R Deploy to staging | C | I | R | I | C UAT sign-off | I | A | I | I | R Production deploy | A | I | R | I | C
example.note: Engineering is A for Code Review (code quality) but R for Write Code — Accountable and Responsible are separated to preserve escalation paths.

example.code: Task | You | Designer | Developer | VA ------------|------|----------|-----------|---- Vision | A/R | I | I | I UI design | A | R | C | I Development | A | C | R | I Testing | A | I | C | R Launch | A/R | I | I | C
example.note: Even with small teams, RACI prevents "I thought you were handling that" disputes with contractors who have parallel commitments.

antipattern.description: A=R for the senior role on every task.
antipattern.reason: LLMs and inexperienced PMs assign A=R to the project lead because charters say "PM owns delivery". The Accountable should not be doing the work — that conflation breaks the escalation chain.

antipattern.description: Assigning C to every named role "to be thorough".
antipattern.reason: Every C requires a feedback round-trip. A matrix with 8 Consulted per task is a meeting schedule, not a responsibility model. Set an explicit cap (3-4 C per task) in the generation prompt.

antipattern.description: Storing RACI only in Confluence with no version history.
antipattern.reason: Role changes are invisible without diffs. Keep RACI in source control as CSV; generate Markdown view for Confluence. Agents should output a unified diff when updating, not a full rewrite.

antipattern.description: Agent silently fixes violations by reassigning roles without human review.
antipattern.reason: Every role change has organizational implications. Violations must surface to the human Accountable for resolution — the agent flags but never resolves autonomously.
